Steps.Name: Print Spooler Service
Steps.Description: Verifies the Print Spooler service is running. This service is required for all printing.

ResolutionText.Failed:
1. Press Win+R, type 'services.msc', find 'Print Spooler'
2. Right-click > Properties, set Startup type to Automatic
3. Click Start, then OK
4. If it fails to start, clear the spooler queue: Stop Spooler, delete files in C:\Windows\System32\spool\PRINTERS\, then Start Spooler

TestResultText.Passed: Print Spooler service is running normally.
TestResultText.Failed: Print Spooler is not running. No print jobs can be processed until it is restarted.
TestResultText.Warning: Print Spooler state is unexpected. Printing may be unreliable.

Steps.Name: Printers Installed
Steps.Description: Checks that at least one printer is installed and not in an error state.

ResolutionText.Failed:
1. Go to Settings > Bluetooth & devices > Printers & scanners
2. Click 'Add device' and follow the wizard
3. For network printers, ensure you are connected to the corporate network or VPN
4. Contact IT if you require a managed printer to be pushed to your device
ResolutionText.Warning:
1. Open Settings > Bluetooth & devices > Printers & scanners
2. Click the printer in error and select 'Open print queue'
3. Cancel all stuck jobs, then try printing again
4. If the issue persists, remove and re-add the printer

TestResultText.Passed: All installed printers are in a healthy state.
TestResultText.Failed: No printers are installed on this device.
TestResultText.Warning: One or more printers are reporting an error state.

Steps.Name: Default Printer Set
Steps.Description: Confirms a default printer is assigned.
Steps.UserFeedback: Checking for a default printer assignment...

ResolutionText.Warning:
1. Go to Settings > Bluetooth & devices > Printers & scanners
2. Click the printer you use most often
3. Select 'Set as default'
4. Alternatively, disable 'Let Windows manage my default printer' and set it manually

TestResultText.Warning: No default printer is set. Applications may prompt you to choose a printer every time.

Steps.Name: Print Spooler Queue
Steps.Description: Checks for stuck jobs in the print queue that may be blocking all printing.
Steps.UserFeedback: Scanning print queue for stuck jobs...
Steps.ResolutionScript: Stop-Service Spooler -Force; Remove-Item "$env:SystemRoot\System32\spool\PRINTERS\*" -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; Start-Service Spooler

ResolutionText.Failed:
1. Open an elevated Command Prompt
2. Run: net stop spooler
3. Run: del /Q /F /S "%systemroot%\System32\spool\PRINTERS\*.*"
4. Run: net start spooler
5. Try printing again
ResolutionText.Warning:
1. Open Settings > Bluetooth & devices > Printers & scanners
2. Click the active printer and select 'Open print queue'
3. Right-click each job and select Cancel

TestResultText.Passed: Print queue is clear with no stuck jobs.
TestResultText.Failed: One or more print jobs are stuck and blocking the queue.
TestResultText.Warning: Print jobs are queued but not confirmed stuck. Printing may be slow.

Steps.Name: Printer Driver Health
Steps.Description: Checks for any printer drivers flagged as corrupted or missing.

ResolutionText.Warning:
1. Open Print Management (printmanagement.msc) as Administrator
2. Expand Drivers and look for any with a warning icon
3. Right-click the bad driver and choose 'Delete'
4. Re-add the printer or push the driver via IT management tools
5. For enterprise managed printers, re-run the printer deployment GPO or contact IT

TestResultText.Passed: All printer drivers are installed and appear healthy.
TestResultText.Failed: Printer driver issues detected that may prevent printing.
TestResultText.Warning: Driver enumeration returned unexpected results. Manually review drivers in Print Management.

Steps.Name: Network Printer Connectivity
Steps.Description: Attempts to verify connectivity to any mapped network printers.
Steps.UserFeedback: Checking reachability of network printer hosts...

ResolutionText.Failed:
1. Verify you are connected to the corporate network or VPN
2. Try pinging the printer IP from Command Prompt: ping <printer-ip>
3. If on VPN, some direct IP printers may not be accessible — use a print server path instead
4. Contact IT to verify the printer IP has not changed or been reassigned
ResolutionText.Warning: Only USB or locally attached printers detected. Network printing requires corporate network connectivity.

TestResultText.Passed: All detected network printers are reachable on the network.
TestResultText.Failed: One or more network printers cannot be reached. Check network or VPN connectivity.
TestResultText.Warning: No network printers detected. Only local or USB printers are present.

Steps.Name: Windows Print & Scan Feature
Steps.Description: Checks that the optional Windows Print and Document Services feature is enabled.

ResolutionText.Failed:
1. Open an elevated PowerShell window
2. Run: Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName 'Printing-Foundation-Features' -NoRestart
3. Restart the computer if prompted
4. Contact IT if you lack permissions to enable Windows features

TestResultText.Passed: Windows Printing Foundation Features are enabled.
TestResultText.Failed: Core printing features are disabled. This will prevent printing from working.
TestResultText.Warning: Unable to confirm printing feature state. If printing is broken, contact IT.
